commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blockers are live proof and maintainer acceptance of source-management/security semantics, not a narrow automated repair.; Needs attention: The branch introduces a new GitHub webhook and archive-to-publish trust boundary that still needs live validation and maintainer acceptance. (sha=ada7cf2267c7cef9e7e057cd1aadf9d0d1318991)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds GitHub App-backed publisher repository sync, source-managed publishing boundaries, an internal rollout UI, historical version scan gates, and a maintainer spec.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new GitHub App sync capability rather than fixing a reproduced bug. The merge-relevant verification is live runtime proof of the new install, webhook, sync, and source-managed publish paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are live proof and maintainer acceptance of source-management/security semantics, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat: GitHub App skill sync behind internal UI gate
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a GitHub App install/setup flow for publishers with HMAC-signed setup state, single-use state consumption, webhook signature verification, and installation webhook claims for org-account handshakes.
- Add repository links, source-link mappings, sync jobs, webhook delivery tracking, and source-managed publish enforcement so only linked skills sync while manual org skills remain untouched.
- Add repository sync from pinned GitHub archive commits, push-job coalescing/serialization, repository removal/rename handling, and source provenance checks.
- Gate the GitHub Sync UI for internal rollout: controls are only shown to users who are owners/admins of the target org publisher and members of the `openclaw` ClawHub publisher.
- Enforce version-level scan gates for explicit historical skill artifact access so a superseded malicious or pending ClawScan/VT version cannot be downloaded or served just because the current latest version is active.
- Document the source-management invariants, version security boundary, GitHub App operational setup, and internal UI rollout gate in `specs/github-app-skill-sync.md`.

## GitHub App setup notes

Required env:

- `GITHUB_APP_ID`
- `GITHUB_APP_SLUG`
- `GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY`
- `GITHUB_APP_WEBHOOK_SECRET`
- optional `GITHUB_APP_STATE_SECRET`

App permissions/events:

- Contents: read-only
- Metadata: read-only
- Webhook URL: `/api/webhooks/github-app`
- Events: `push`, `installation`, `installation_repositories`, `repository`

Repository links are disabled by default after installation until an org admin enables sync. Org install completion requires a signed GitHub installation webhook claim matching the current user's GitHub account; if the redirect beats the webhook, retry completion after the webhook arrives.

During internal rollout, the UI only exposes GitHub Sync controls when the viewer is both an owner/admin of the viewed org publisher and a member of the `openclaw` ClawHub publisher. This is a UI/discoverability gate; backend GitHub sync functions still enforce the target publisher admin boundary.

## Security boundary note

GitHub sync publishes through the normal skill publish pipeline and queues the normal scanners for each new `skillVersions` row. Scanner completion only promotes verdicts to the skill-level moderation row when the scanned version is still latest, which prevents stale async results from overwriting the current latest verdict.

Because historical versions remain addressable by explicit `version` or `tag`, artifact-serving endpoints now also enforce the requested version's own ClawScan/VT fields. Download, card, raw file, and version-detail endpoints block malicious or explicitly pending historical versions; static-scan-only findings remain advisory when ClawScan/VT do not block the version; scan and verification endpoints remain readable so callers can inspect the blocked version's security context.

## Maintainer acceptance callouts

This PR introduces source-managed behavior only for skills that an org explicitly opts into GitHub sync:

- Existing manual org skills remain manual unless they get an active source link for a linked repository/path.
- Once a skill has an active source link, manual publishes to that source-managed skill are rejected unless the publish comes from the matching source link and repository/path provenance.
- To return a source-managed skill to manual publishing, an org admin must disable/unlink the source link first; disabled source links are no longer authoritative.
- Repository removal, installation deletion, and installation suspension disable affected repository/source links without touching unrelated manual org skills.
- Repository links are disabled by default after app installation; an org admin must explicitly enable each repo before sync can publish.

Maintainers should explicitly accept these source-management, unlink, and rollback semantics before merge. The remaining ClawSweeper blocker is live proof from a real configured GitHub App install/webhook/sync path.

## Review feedback addressed

- Vercel inline feedback: bounded `fetchGitHubInstallationRepositories` pagination at 100 pages / 10,000 repositories and fail closed if GitHub keeps returning full pages.
- ClawSweeper code feedback: restored shared `SkillCard` title/summary-before-badges ordering and added a regression test for that order.
- Internal rollout feedback: added a UI-only OpenClaw membership gate around the GitHub Sync panel and callback completion effect, with regression coverage for allowed and hidden cases.
- ClawSweeper proof feedback: added a tracked runtime proof checklist to `specs/github-app-skill-sync.md` for the real GitHub App validation pass once app credentials exist; local `.env.local` does not currently contain the required GitHub App credentials to honestly capture live install/webhook proof from this worktree.
- ClawSweeper proof feedback: automated proof is below. Full real install/webhook runtime proof still requires creating/configuring the actual GitHub App and secrets; this PR is ready for that app-creation validation pass.

## Proof / validation

Latest pushed SHA: `ada7cf22`.

Current-head CI on `ada7cf22` was observed passing for `static`, `unit`, `types-build`, `packages`, `e2e-http`, `playwright-smoke`, `playwright-local-auth`, CodeQL, Vercel preview/agent review, repository dispatch, auto-response, and secret scanning. Older cancelled `auto-response`/`dispatch` entries are superseded runs from prior heads; the latest current-head entries are green.

Local commands run:

- `bunx vitest run src/__tests__/publisher-github-sync-panel.test.tsx --reporter=dot` — 1 file / 5 tests passed.
- `bun run format:check -- 'src/routes/user/$handle.tsx' src/__tests__/publisher-github-sync-panel.test.tsx specs/github-app-skill-sync.md` — passed.
- `bun run lint` — passed.
- `bunx vitest run src/components/SkillCard.test.tsx src/__tests__/publisher-github-sync-panel.test.tsx convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 3 files / 11 tests passed.
- `bunx vitest run convex/downloads.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 2 files / 258 tests passed.
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` — passed.
- `bun run lint:oxlint -- ./convex/githubApp.ts ./src/components/SkillCard.tsx ./src/components/SkillCard.test.tsx` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- `bun run format:check -- specs/github-app-skill-sync.md` — passed.
- `bunx vitest run convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 1 file / 7 tests passed.
- `bun run format:check -- convex/lib/githubAppSync.ts convex/lib/githubCommon.ts convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts` — passed.
- `bun run lint:oxlint -- ./convex/lib/githubAppSync.ts ./convex/lib/githubCommon.ts ./convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts` — passed.
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` — passed.

GitHub sync publishes through the normal skill publish pipeline and queues the normal scanners for each new `skillVersions` row. Scanner completion only promotes verdicts to the skill-level moderation row when the scanned version is still latest, which prevents stale async results from overwriting the current latest verdict.

Because historical versions remain addressable by explicit `version` or `tag`, artifact-serving endpoints now also enforce the requested version's own ClawScan/VT fields. Download, card, raw file, and version-detail endpoints block malicious or explicitly pending historical versions; static-scan-only findings remain advisory when ClawScan/VT do not block the version; scan and verification endpoints remain readable so callers can inspect the blocked version's security context.

## Maintainer acceptance callouts

This PR introduces source-managed behavior only for skills that an org explicitly opts into GitHub sync:

- Existing manual org skills remain manual unless they get an active source link for a linked repository/path.
- Once a skill has an active source link, manual publishes to that source-managed skill are rejected unless the publish comes from the matching source link and repository/path provenance.
- To return a source-managed skill to manual publishing, an org admin must disable/unlink the source link first; disabled source links are no longer authoritative.
- Repository removal, installation deletion, and installation suspension disable affected repository/source links without touching unrelated manual org skills.
- Repository links are disabled by default after app installation; an org admin must explicitly enable each repo before sync can publish.

Maintainers should explicitly accept these source-management, unlink, and rollback semantics before merge. The remaining ClawSweeper blocker is live proof from a real configured GitHub App install/webhook/sync path.

## Review feedback addressed

- Vercel inline feedback: bounded `fetchGitHubInstallationRepositories` pagination at 100 pages / 10,000 repositories and fail closed if GitHub keeps returning full pages.
- ClawSweeper code feedback: restored shared `SkillCard` title/summary-before-badges ordering and added a regression test for that order.
- Internal rollout feedback: added a UI-only OpenClaw membership gate around the GitHub Sync panel and callback completion effect, with regression coverage for allowed and hidden cases.
- ClawSweeper proof feedback: added a tracked runtime proof checklist to `specs/github-app-skill-sync.md` for the real GitHub App validation pass once app credentials exist; local `.env.local` does not currently contain the required GitHub App credentials to honestly capture live install/webhook proof from this worktree.
- ClawSweeper proof feedback: automated proof is below. Full real install/webhook runtime proof still requires creating/configuring the actual GitHub App and secrets; this PR is ready for that app-creation validation pass.

## Proof / validation

Latest pushed SHA: `ada7cf22`.

Current-head CI on `ada7cf22` was observed passing for `static`, `unit`, `types-build`, `packages`, `e2e-http`, `playwright-smoke`, `playwright-local-auth`, CodeQL, Vercel preview/agent review, repository dispatch, auto-response, and secret scanning. Older cancelled `auto-response`/`dispatch` entries are superseded runs from prior heads; the latest current-head entries are green.

- `bunx vitest run src/components/SkillCard.test.tsx src/__tests__/publisher-github-sync-panel.test.tsx convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 3 files / 11 tests passed.

target.title: feat: GitHub App skill sync behind internal UI gate
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a GitHub App install/setup flow for publishers with HMAC-signed setup state, single-use state consumption, webhook signature verification, and installation webhook claims for org-account handshakes.
- Add repository links, source-link mappings, sync jobs, webhook delivery tracking, and source-managed publish enforcement so only linked skills sync while manual org skills remain untouched.
- Add repository sync from pinned GitHub archive commits, push-job coalescing/serialization, repository removal/rename handling, and source provenance checks.
- Gate the GitHub Sync UI for internal rollout: controls are only shown to users who are owners/admins of the target org publisher and members of the `openclaw` ClawHub publisher.
- Enforce version-level scan gates for explicit historical skill artifact access so a superseded malicious or pending ClawScan/VT version cannot be downloaded or served just because the current latest version is active.
- Document the source-management invariants, version security boundary, GitHub App operational setup, and internal UI rollout gate in `specs/github-app-skill-sync.md`.

## GitHub App setup notes

Required env:

- `GITHUB_APP_ID`
- `GITHUB_APP_SLUG`
- `GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY`
- `GITHUB_APP_WEBHOOK_SECRET`
- optional `GITHUB_APP_STATE_SECRET`

App permissions/events:

- Contents: read-only
- Metadata: read-only
- Webhook URL: `/api/webhooks/github-app`
- Events: `push`, `installation`, `installation_repositories`, `repository`

Repository links are disabled by default after installation until an org admin enables sync. Org install completion requires a signed GitHub installation webhook claim matching the current user's GitHub account; if the redirect beats the webhook, retry completion after the webhook arrives.

During internal rollout, the UI only exposes GitHub Sync controls when the viewer is both an owner/admin of the viewed org publisher and a member of the `openclaw` ClawHub publisher. This is a UI/discoverability gate; backend GitHub sync functions still enforce the target publisher admin boundary.

## Security boundary note

GitHub sync publishes through the normal skill publish pipeline and queues the normal scanners for each new `skillVersions` row. Scanner completion only promotes verdicts to the skill-level moderation row when the scanned version is still latest, which prevents stale async results from overwriting the current latest verdict.

Because historical versions remain addressable by explicit `version` or `tag`, artifact-serving endpoints now also enforce the requested version's own ClawScan/VT fields. Download, card, raw file, and version-detail endpoints block malicious or explicitly pending historical versions; static-scan-only findings remain advisory when ClawScan/VT do not block the version; scan and verification endpoints remain readable so callers can inspect the blocked version's security context.

## Maintainer acceptance callouts

This PR introduces source-managed behavior only for skills that an org explicitly opts into GitHub sync:

- Existing manual org skills remain manual unless they get an active source link for a linked repository/path.
- Once a skill has an active source link, manual publishes to that source-managed skill are rejected unless the publish comes from the matching source link and repository/path provenance.
- To return a source-managed skill to manual publishing, an org admin must disable/unlink the source link first; disabled source links are no longer authoritative.
- Repository removal, installation deletion, and installation suspension disable affected repository/source links without touching unrelated manual org skills.
- Repository links are disabled by default after app installation; an org admin must explicitly enable each repo before sync can publish.

Maintainers should explicitly accept these source-management, unlink, and rollback semantics before merge. The remaining ClawSweeper blocker is live proof from a real configured GitHub App install/webhook/sync path.

## Review feedback addressed

- Vercel inline feedback: bounded `fetchGitHubInstallationRepositories` pagination at 100 pages / 10,000 repositories and fail closed if GitHub keeps returning full pages.
- ClawSweeper code feedback: restored shared `SkillCard` title/summary-before-badges ordering and added a regression test for that order.
- Internal rollout feedback: added a UI-only OpenClaw membership gate around the GitHub Sync panel and callback completion effect, with regression coverage for allowed and hidden cases.
- ClawSweeper proof feedback: added a tracked runtime proof checklist to `specs/github-app-skill-sync.md` for the real GitHub App validation pass once app credentials exist; local `.env.local` does not currently contain the required GitHub App credentials to honestly capture live install/webhook proof from this worktree.
- ClawSweeper proof feedback: automated proof is below. Full real install/webhook runtime proof still requires creating/configuring the actual GitHub App and secrets; this PR is ready for that app-creation validation pass.

## Proof / validation

Older cancelled `auto-response`/`dispatch` entries are superseded runs from prior heads; the latest current-head entries are green.
